name: Laie
desc: BYU-Hawaii and Polynesian Cultural Center town — LDS community hub, college-town energy, safe and tidy.
insider: Polynesian Cultural Center evening luau and show is world-class — buy the daytime-only ticket for a budget-friendly cultural experience
Hukilau Beach is the local swim spot — calm turquoise water and far less crowded than any beach in Honolulu
The BYU-Hawaii campus is open, beautifully maintained, and great for a peaceful walk — the art exhibits are free
Laie Point has dramatic blowholes, a sea arch, and crashing waves — it's a short walk from the road with no entrance fee
The town is heavily LDS (Mormon) influenced — very safe, very clean, with strong community values and low crime
Angel's Ice Cream and Hukilau Marketplace have affordable eats and local goods — the food court is solid for the price
The commute to Honolulu is 50-70 minutes depending on traffic — most residents work locally at PCC or BYU-Hawaii
Laie Shopping Center is modest but covers basics — for serious grocery shopping, head to Hauula or Kaneohe
The Laie Hawaii Temple is stunning from the outside and the grounds are open to visitors — a beautiful, peaceful landmark
Gunstock Ranch offers horseback riding along the coast and into the mountains — one of the best trail ride experiences on Oahu
